metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4577
metadata.comment:
Rationale: DIP2A is involved in acetylated coenzyme A (Ac-CoA) synthesis and is primarily expressed in the brain regions with abundant pyramidal neurons.

Figure 1C: Western blots of isolated synaptosome fractions from murine brain detected the expression of DIP2A in PSD fraction (PSD marker: PSD95)

Figure 1D: Immunostaining of cultured neurons with an antibody against DIP2A (produced by the authors) showed localization of DIP2A at dendritic shafts and spines at DIV18 as well as partial colocalization with PSD95.

Figure S3B: Western blot demonstrating the specificity of the antibody against DIP2A in the Dip2a KO mice. Experimental description: Dip2a KO mice were generated using CRISPR/Cas9 technology and backcrossed with C57BL/6 over 9 generations. Heterozygous Dip2a KO mice were crossed for producing offspring. Littermate mice of WT and KO genotypes were allocated randomly for experiments. Primary cortical neurons were isolated from P0 pups.

antibody;
"In the present study, we generated an antibody against DIP2A (anti-DIP2A, S1A Fig) and confirmed its expression in the brain from the embryonic to the adult stage (S1B and S1C Fig)."
